customer_name: Laredo Safari and Adventure Park LLC
site_name: Laredo Safari and Adventure Park LLC

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The licensee did not have a written, documented program of veterinary care established and maintained under the
supervision of an attending veterinarian for the animals housed at this facility for the past year. During the inspection, the
licensee could not produce any program of veterinary care, written documentation of veterinary oversight or records
showing that a veterinarian has been contacted or designated to provide services for these animals. Failure to establish
and maintain a program of veterinary care places the animals at risk of undetected illness, injury, and inadequate medical
treatment. Without veterinary involvement the health and welfare of the animals cannot be assured. The licensee must
establish a written program of veterinary care that includes the designation of an attending veterinarian who is readily
available to provide adequate veterinary care. The program must include regularly scheduled visits, provisions for
emergency care, preventative medical practices, and documentation of treatments provided.
CORRECTION DATE: 17 APR 2026

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
There was a flea infestation that occurred at the facility during February 2026.
One female pig tailed monkey, 25 year old, named “Yaya” was affected by the fleas. She received antiparasitic treatment
without direction from the attending veterinarian and ended up passing. Other animals (goats, lemurs, chickens, serval,
baboons, kangaroos, goose, llama, and alpaca) affected by the fleas, were also treated with antiparasitic treatment
without direction of the attending veterinarian and the issue resolved following treatment. Without veterinarian oversight,
the diagnosis as well as the methods used to care for the animals could be inadequate or inappropriate for the animal.
The licensee, in conjunction with a veterinarian, must use appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat
diseases and injuries, and must determine the availability of emergency, weekend, and holiday care.
CORRECTION DATE: 17 APRIL 2026

It was reported to the Webb County Sheriffs Office of a lemur bite on January 21, 2026. This incident occurred when the
individual went to the lemur island at the facility and the was bitten by a lemur while feeding it. Allowing public interactions
with nonhuman primates without direct control can lead to harm to the animals or the public. Trained nonhuman primates
may be allowed physical contact with the public, but only if they are under the direct control and supervision of an
experienced handler or trainer at all times during the contact.
CORRECTION DATE: 08 APR 2026

At the petting zoo area, a capybara in an outdoor enclosure died due to cold weather in January 2026. Inadequate
provisions from local climatic conditions could subject the species to unfavorable weather, affecting the health of the
animals and creating unnecessary discomfort or could lead to death. Natural or artificial shelter appropriate to the local
climatic conditions for the species concerned shall be provided for all animals kept outdoors to afford them protection and
to prevent discomfort to such animals.
CORRECTION DATE: 10 APRIL 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
Additional Inspectors:
Alexandra Calderon, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.40(b) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The licensee did not have a written, documented program of veterinary care established and maintained under the 
supervision of an attending veterinarian for the animals housed at this facility for the past year. During the inspection, the

establish a written program of veterinary care that includes the designation of an attending veterinarian who is readily 
available to provide adequate veterinary care. The program must include regularly scheduled visits, provisions for 
emergency care, preventative medical practices, and documentation of treatments provided. 
 
CORRECTION DATE: 17 APR 2026 
2.40(b)(2)  Critical
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
There was a flea infestation that occurred at the facility during February 2026.  
One female pig tailed monkey, 25 year old, named “Yaya” was affected by the fleas. She received antiparasitic treatment 
without direction from the attending veterinarian and ended up passing. Other animals (goats, lemurs, chickens, serval, 
baboons, kangaroos, goose, llama, and alpaca) affected by the fleas, were also treated with antiparasitic treatment

the diagnosis as well as the methods used to care for the animals could be inadequate or inappropriate for the animal. 
The licensee, in conjunction with a veterinarian, must use appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat 
diseases and injuries, and must determine the availability of emergency, weekend, and holiday care.  
 
CORRECTION DATE: 17 APRIL 2026 
3.78(e) 
   
Outdoor housing facilities.

It was reported to the Webb County Sheriffs Office of a lemur bite on January 21, 2026. This incident occurred when the 
individual went to the lemur island at the facility and the was bitten by a lemur while feeding it. Allowing public interactions 
with nonhuman primates without direct control can lead to harm to the animals or the public. Trained nonhuman primates 
may be allowed physical contact with the public, but only if they are under the direct control and supervision of an 
experienced handler or trainer at all times during the contact.  
 
CORRECTION DATE: 08 APR 2026 
3.127(b)  Critical
   
Facilities, outdoor. 
At the petting zoo area, a capybara in an outdoor enclosure died due to cold weather in January 2026. Inadequate 
provisions from local climatic conditions could subject the species to unfavorable weather, affecting the health of the 
animals and creating unnecessary discomfort or could lead to death. Natural or artificial shelter appropriate to the local 
climatic conditions for the species concerned shall be provided for all animals kept outdoors to afford them protection and

CORRECTION DATE: 10 APRIL 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
